creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
I generally just feel a bit sorry for street scammers as they’re probably not “working” their dream job, but when things get physical they’ve crossed the line ❌

Also, I know Paris gets a lot of tourists and that the locals probably don’t go anywhere near Sacré Coeur, but I think it’s crazy that the authorities have just give up and that they’re fine with the way visitors to Paris are being treated here at one of the most famous places in the country’s capital… 

I’ve also just seen a video by the @realhonestguide where they got attacked and followed by a group of scammers right beside the Eiffel Tower… What’s going on, Paris? 😅

Have any of you guys experienced the same or was I just super unlucky this time around? 🧐

#paris #sacrecoeur #paristourisme

transcription.text: Paris you have a big problem and it's frankly embarrassing. I've been visiting Paris for the past 10 years and I feel like the scammers have become much more aggressive, especially the dudes at the foot of Sacré-Cœur who try to put a little string on your hand and then demand money. The other day they grabbed my girlfriend and me who were just trying to walk past and ignore them. We kept walking and told them to stop touching us but the two main dudes just wouldn't let go and there were around 10 other guys who got near us until my girlfriend literally slapped their hands away So here we are at one of the most famous attractions in France and you literally have to fight your way out. I remember the days when you can just walk past them but they've definitely become more aggressive and of course the police were nowhere to be seen even though this happens every day. Now I like Paris but it's embarrassing that the authorities allow stuff like this in a capital city in Europe. I would definitely feel so ashamed if this happened in my city.
